title: The Introduction of Domestic Camels to the Southern Levant: Evidence from the Aravah Valley (Tel Aviv 40)
author: Lidar Sapir-Hen; Erez Ben-Yosef
publisher: Tel Aviv: Journal of the Institute of Archaeology of Tel Aviv University
allowedUseNotes: Copyrighted journal article. Summaries in project's own words only.
summary: Zooarchaeological argument that domestic dromedaries entered the southern Levant in large numbers only in the late 10th century BC. Directly relevant to how the Amalekites' camels (1 Sam 30:17) should be depicted and annotated.
confidenceNotes: Strong for the Aravah copper-district record; extrapolation to desert-fringe raiders is debated — keep both views visible.
